questions.stem: For the system shown in Fig. Q1, find N1/N2 so that the settling time (2% tolerance band) for a step torque input is 16 seconds. Also, find the value of damping ratio, natural frequency and static gain of the system for this value of N1/N2. The system has a primary inertia of 1 kg-m² connected via gear ratio N1/N2 to a secondary shaft with 1 kg-m² inertia, a spring of 1 N-m/rad, and a damper of 1 N-m-s/rad.
common_errors: Forgetting to reflect inertia and spring/damper constants through gear ratio squared
Using wrong settling time formula (ts = 4/ζωn for 2% criterion)
Not computing static gain of the resulting system
questions.solution_method: Reflect all secondary-side parameters (inertia, spring, damper) to the primary shaft using (N1/N2)² scaling to get an equivalent 2nd-order system. Use ts = 4/(ζωn) = 16 s to establish a constraint, then solve for N1/N2. Back-compute ζ, ωn, and static gain K = 1/k_eq.

questions.stem: A motor and generator are set up to drive a load as shown in Fig. Q2. The generator output voltage is e_g(t) = K_f * i_f(t). For the generator: K_f = 2Ω, motor: K_t = 1 N-m/A, K_b = 1 V-s/rad. The motor armature inertia J_a = 0.75 kg-m², armature resistance 1Ω, field circuit resistance 1Ω and inductance 1H. Load inertia 4 kg-m², gear ratio 1:10 between motor and load, load damping 4 N-m-s/rad. Find the transfer function G(s) = θ_o(s)/E_i(s) in zero-pole-gain form.
common_errors: Forgetting to reflect load inertia and damping through gear ratio squared to motor shaft
Missing back-EMF term in armature voltage equation
Not accounting for field circuit dynamics (1H inductance adds a pole)
questions.solution_method: Write field circuit equation: E_i(s) = I_f(s)(1 + s). Then E_g(s) = K_f * I_f(s). Write armature circuit: E_g(s) = I_a(s)(R_a + R_arm) + K_b * s * Θ_m(s). Torque equation: K_t * I_a(s) = (J_eq * s² + B_eq * s) * Θ_m(s) where J_eq and B_eq include reflected load. Θ_o = Θ_m / 10. Cascade all sub-TFs and simplify to zero-pole-gain form.
key_formulas: Field circuit: E_i(s) = I_f(s) * (R_f + L_f * s) = I_f(s)(1 + s)
Armature: E_g(s) = I_a(s)(R_a + R_motor) + K_b * s * Θ_m(s)
Torque: T_m(s) = K_t * I_a(s) = J_eq * s^2 * Θ_m(s) + B_eq * s * Θ_m(s)
J_eq = J_a + J_load/n^2, B_eq = B_load/n^2, n=10

questions.stem: Using the motor-generator system of Q2(a), θ_o is to be controlled with a unity-feedback loop and proportional controller with gain K_c. Find K_c to achieve a steady-state error of 0.01 rad for a ramp reference input. Assume feedback gain unity.
common_errors: Using position error constant Kp instead of velocity error constant Kv for ramp input
Not accounting for existing plant poles at origin when computing Kv
Forgetting to multiply by K_c in the open-loop gain
questions.solution_method: Identify system type from G(s) obtained in Q2(a). For a ramp input, ess = 1/Kv where Kv = lim[s→0] s * K_c * G(s). Set ess = 0.01 and solve for K_c.

questions.stem: Consider the position servomechanism in Fig. Q3 with forward path: K1 → K_A → K/[s(τs+1)], unity feedback. Given τ = 0.02 s, K = 0.1, K1 = 1. Find ωn and ζ for amplifier gain K_A = 500 V/V. What is the steady-state error for a unit step and a unit ramp input?
common_errors: Misidentifying system type (type 1 → zero sse for step, finite for ramp)
Incorrect closed-loop CE formation from cascaded blocks
Computing ωn and ζ from open-loop rather than closed-loop characteristic equation
questions.solution_method: Open-loop TF: G(s) = K1*K_A*K / [s(τs+1)] = 0.1*500 / [s(0.02s+1)] = 50/[s(0.02s+1)]. Closed-loop CE: 0.02s² + s + 50 = 0, i.e., s² + 50s + 2500 = 0. Read off ωn = 50 rad/s, ζ = 0.5. System is type 1: ess(step) = 0, ess(ramp) = 1/Kv = 1/50 = 0.02 rad.

questions.stem: For the servomechanism of Q3, a PD controller with transfer function (K_d/K_A * s + 1) is added in the forward path. Find K_d such that the closed-loop dynamics have a relative damping ratio of unity (critically damped).
common_errors: Forgetting the PD controller introduces a zero, not just extra damping in CE
Setting ζ = 1 without re-deriving CE with the PD term
Algebraic errors in expanding the modified CE
questions.solution_method: With PD controller C(s) = (K_d/K_A)*s + 1, the open-loop becomes G_c(s)*G(s) = K1*K_A*K*((K_d/K_A)s+1)/[s(τs+1)]. Closed-loop CE: τs² + (1 + K1*K*K_d)s + K1*K_A*K = 0. For ζ=1: (1 + K1*K*K_d)² = 4τ*K1*K_A*K. Solve for K_d.
key_formulas: CE with PD: τs² + (1 + K*K1*K_d)s + K1*K_A*K = 0

notes: Tutorial dated 3 March 2026. Three questions total; Q2 and Q3 each have two parts extracted as separate entries. All problems involve electromechanical modelling and time-domain analysis. No marks assigned (tutorial, not graded).
